sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Omnicom Doubles Its Cost-Saving Target to $1.5 Billion
feed.summary: Omnicom, now the world's largest advertising holding company after acquiring IPG, announced a 10.1% revenue growth to $17.3 billion in 2025. Despite a net loss of $54.5 million due to acquisition costs, the company has doubled its cost synergy target to $1.5 billion, with $900 million aimed for 2026. This significant saving will be partly reinvested into their Omni data and AI platform, alongside a $5.0 billion share buyback program.

feed.title: Southern Company beats revenue expectations, stock rises despite earnings miss
feed.summary: Southern Company reported fourth-quarter 2025 adjusted earnings of $0.55 per share, missing analyst expectations, but revenue surged to $7.0 billion, exceeding estimates. The company's shares rose 2.47% in pre-market trading, driven by the strong revenue performance and a positive 2026 outlook. For the full year 2025, adjusted earnings reached $4.30 per share with annual revenue climbing to $29.6 billion.

feed.title: Canada Coke bottler invests in Ontario facility
feed.summary: Coca-Cola Canada Bottling is investing C$141m ($102.9m) to expand its manufacturing and distribution facility in Brampton, Ontario. This investment, described as the company's largest to date, will add a new production line, increasing capacity by at least 20 million cases annually and speeding up innovation delivery. The expansion builds on a previous C$8m investment in its Hamilton distribution center and highlights the company's commitment to growth across Ontario and eastern Canada.

feed.title: Pool (NASDAQ:POOL) Misses Q4 CY2025 Revenue Estimates, Stock Drops
feed.summary: Pool Corporation (NASDAQ: POOL) reported Q4 CY2025 revenue of $982.2 million, missing analyst estimates by 1.7%, with sales flat year-on-year. The company's GAAP EPS of $0.85 also fell short of expectations by 12.2%, and its GAAP EPS guidance for FY2026 is below analyst consensus. Following the announcement, the stock dropped by 6.6%.

feed.title: Garrett Motion (NASDAQ:GTX) Surprises With Q4 CY2025 Sales But Stock Drops
feed.summary: Garrett Motion (NASDAQ:GTX) reported better-than-expected Q4 CY2025 sales and GAAP profit, with revenue up 5.6% year-on-year to $891 million. Despite these positive results and an EPS beat, the stock dropped 5.3% immediately following the announcement, suggesting the market might have anticipated even stronger performance. The company provided a 2026 EBITDA guidance in line with analyst expectations and highlighted its focus on a disciplined capital allocation framework.

feed.title: Iron Mountain partners with energy storage firm Calibrant to deploy 23MWh BESS unit at New Jersey data center
feed.summary: Iron Mountain has partnered with Calibrant Energy to deploy a 23MWh battery energy storage system (BESS) at its New Jersey data center. This BESS will integrate with an existing 7.2MW rooftop solar installation and aims to stabilize the regional grid during peak demand while improving the economic and operational efficiency of the local utility grid. The project highlights how the data center industry can contribute to solving emerging grid challenges.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin to provide C-130J training devices and upgrades to RAAF
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin has secured a contract to supply the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) with C-130J training devices and upgrades, with deliveries expected to begin in 2029. The agreement includes two Weapon Systems Trainers, a Loadmaster Part-Task Trainer, an Enhanced Integrated Cockpit Systems Trainer (EICS), and enhancements to existing virtual simulation and maintenance trainers. These additions will support the RAAF's acquisition of new C-130J-30 aircraft and provide advanced, realistic training for aircrews.

feed.title: News Corp Highlights $1 Billion Share Repurchase Program
feed.summary: News Corp has announced a $1 billion share repurchase program for its Nasdaq-listed Class A and B common stock, aiming to enhance shareholder value. Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC will execute the buybacks, with the company providing daily transaction disclosures to the ASX. While the AI Analyst, Spark, rates NWSA as Neutral due to mixed fundamentals and technicals, a recent analyst rating suggests a Buy with a $25.00 price target.

feed.title: Honeywell International CEO: Q1 Tracking to Guidance as Aerospace, Buildings Surge and Split Nears
feed.summary: Honeywell's CEO Vimal Kapur reported that Q1 2026 performance is tracking to guidance, driven by strong growth in Building Automation and Aerospace, despite uneven industrial demand in Europe and China. The company is actively working on its planned separation into three entities, focusing on becoming a pure-play automation company by addressing capacity constraints, refining pricing strategies against persistent inflation, and advancing its Quantinuum quantum computing venture.

feed.title: Synchrony Launches First-of-Its-Kind Social App to Support Neurodivergent Adults in Building Friendship, Community and Independence
feed.summary: Synchrony has launched a new social connection platform designed to help neurodivergent adults form friendships, build confidence, and find belonging. The app focuses on interest-based matching, a verified and moderated community, and offers an optional AI-supported coaching tool named Jesse to help users navigate social interactions. This initiative aims to address the social isolation neurodivergent individuals often experience after leaving structured school environments, providing a safe and supportive space.

feed.title: Boeing rival Airbus’ stock drops after earnings beat. This is the big problem.
feed.summary: This article reports on Airbus's stock drop despite an earnings beat, highlighting a significant underlying problem. The specific reasons for the stock decline and the "big problem" are not detailed in this snippet but would be discussed in the full article.

feed.title: Clean Harbors to Participate in Raymond James Investor Conference on March 3
feed.summary: Clean Harbors, Inc. announced that its Co-Chief Executive Officer Eric W. Gerstenberg, Chief Financial Officer Eric J. Dugas, and SVP Investor Relations Jim Buckley will participate in a fireside chat at the Raymond James & Associates Institutional Investors Conference. The event will be webcast live at 8:05 a.m. ET on Tuesday, March 3, 2026, and can be accessed via the company's investor relations website. Clean Harbors (NYSE: CLH) is a leading provider of environmental and industrial services in North America.

feed.title: Sonali Mathur, Humana Vice President of Data Strategy & Governance
feed.summary: Sonali Mathur, Humana's Vice President of Data Strategy & Governance and Chief Data Governance Officer, has been appointed to CDO Magazine's Global Board. She is recognized for her extensive experience in driving enterprise-wide data transformation, managing data strategy, governance, and protection initiatives at Humana, and leading large-scale data transformation at General Motors. Mathur is also lauded for her commitment to mentoring women and underrepresented talent in STEM.

feed.title: Humana declares quarterly dividend of $0.885 per share
feed.summary: Humana Inc. announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.885 per share, totaling an annual dividend of $3.54 and a 1.89% yield. The dividend is payable on April 24, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 27, 2026, marking 15 consecutive years of dividend payments for the healthcare company. This announcement comes alongside news of Humana's strategic investments in healthcare initiatives and acquisitions, while also facing adjusted price targets and downgrades from analysts due to potential cost pressures.

feed.title: Pineapple Financial Completes $2 Million INJ Purchase
feed.summary: Pineapple Financial Inc. has completed a $2 million open-market purchase of 560,647 Injective (INJ) tokens at an average price of $3.567 per token. This acquisition is part of the company's ongoing digital asset treasury strategy, which now holds $20.79 million in capital reserves for future INJ purchases. Pineapple Financial, which also operates as a mortgage brokerage network, aims to deliver shareholder value through strategic capital allocation and offers a real-time treasury dashboard for transparency.
